description_long: This house features a steeply pitched, shingled gable roof with a prominent central dormer and a smaller gable over the entryway. The exterior walls are finished in a light beige color, complemented by black window shutters and white window frames. The front facade displays a symmetrical arrangement of windows, with a covered porch supported by columns and a set of steps leading to the main entrance. The side elevations are plain, with no windows or decorative elements, while the rear mirrors the front in window arrangement but lacks an entryway. The roof tiles have a textured, weathered appearance, and the overall structure suggests a wood-framed construction with masonry or stucco cladding. The architectural style is reminiscent of traditional American Colonial homes, with clean lines and a balanced, orderly design.
description: A beige, gable-roofed house with black shutters, a central dormer, and a covered front porch with columns. The design is symmetrical and traditional, featuring a shingled roof and multiple windows.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entryway with a covered porch, steps, columns, and symmetrical windows with black shutters.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the plain gable end of the house with no windows or decorative features, and a partial view of the porch roof.
description_view_2: Rear view featuring a symmetrical arrangement of windows similar to the front but without an entryway or porch.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the other plain gable end, mirroring the first side with no windows or decorations.
attribute_entryway_design: Central covered porch with steps, columns, and a gabled overhang
